questionText: Create a class Bookstore, which has an arraylist of book titles (strings). In the constructor, add in the titles "LearningJava" and "Java 101", and use a for-each loop to print all the titles in uppercase.
hints: New hint
New hint

questionText: Create a class Movie with a title that cannot be null, empty, less than six characters, and cannot contain the word "bcit". With any errors, throw IllegalArgumentException with message "WRONG". Use best practices with the code and comments. Create a getter method. Create a Main class with the main() method that creates a Book object and prints its title in uppercase.

Write a class (no JavaDocs required) Dog and a class BadName (a checked exception class). The dog has a String name field. Its constructor stores the name if valid (i.e. not null); otherwise a BadName exception is thrown.
hints: Create a class called BadName that extends Exception.
Create a private String field 'name' in Dog.
Use the Dog constructor to validate the name.
Throw new BadName() if the name is null.
Remember that checked exceptions must be declared in the constructor signature with 'throws BadName'.

questionText:
Create a class called Earth. Assume you already have a list of country names (strings) called "countries". From the main() method, use the intermediate and terminal streaming operations to do the following:

a) collect in a new list: all countries that start with B or C in lowercase, sorted by length
b) print in reverse (do not store in a list) all countries that end with 'y' or 'Y'
c) create a method called getStream() which takes a list of strings as an argument, and returns a stream of strings that are not null, not blank, and are at least five characters long.
hints: Use countries.stream() to begin working with streams.
For part (a), use filter() to check if a string starts with 'b' or 'c'.
Convert strings to lowercase before checking with startsWith().
Use sorted() with a comparator to sort by string length.
Collect results using .toList() or collect(Collectors.toList()).
For part (b), filter using endsWith() and check both 'y' and 'Y'.
Use sorted() with Comparator.reverseOrder() to reverse order without storing.
Use forEach() as the terminal operation to print results.
For part (c), return a stream using list.stream().filter(...).
Use conditions: string != null, !string.isBlank(), and string.length() >= 5.

questionText: Create a Vehicle class. Vehicles have a color (String) and a printDetails() method (prints the color). Create a Car class with a number of doors (int) and a printDetails() method (prints the number of doors). Create an Airplane class with a printDetails() method (prints "I am a plane"). Show the code for both classes. Then write the main() method which shows substitution and polymorphism (overriding).
hints: Create a base class Vehicle with a private String color.
Add a constructor in Vehicle to initialize the color.
Create a method of printDetails() in Vehicle that prints the color.
Make Car extend Vehicle using 'extends'.
Add an int field for number of doors in Car.
Override printDetails() in Car to print the number of doors.
Create Airplane as a subclass of Vehicle.
Override printDetails() in Airplane to print "I am a plane".
In main(), use a Vehicle reference to store Car and Airplane objects.
Call printDetails() on each object to demonstrate polymorphism.

questionText: Show a class called Dog which has fields for name and year of birth. Include a constructor and methods that validate the fields: the name cannot be null or empty, and the year of birth must be less than 2027. Comment your code.
hints: Create private fields: String name and int yearOfBirth.
Use a constructor to initialize both fields.
Check if name is null or empty using conditions.
Throw an IllegalArgumentException if the name is valid.
Check if yearOfBirth is less than 2027.
Throw an IllegalArgumentException if the year is invalid.
Add getter methods for both fields.
Include comments explaining validation logic and methods.

questionText: Create an interface called Reservable that represents something that can be reserved. It must include: a method that indicates whether the item is currently reserved, a method that reserves the item, and a method that cancels the reservation.
hints: Use the keyword 'interface' instead of 'class'.
Interface methods do not have a body.
Create a method that returns a boolean to indicate reservation status.
Name the method something like isReserved().
Create a method reserve() with no return value.
Create a method cancelReservation() with no return value.
Remember that methods in an interface are public by default.

questionText: Create a class HotelRoom that implements Reservable and Comparable. HotelRoom has a roomNumber, starts out as not reserved, becomes reserved when reserved, becomes not reserved when the reservation is canceled. Hotel rooms with higher room numbers titles are "bigger".
hints: Use 'implements Reservable, Comparable<HotelRoom>' in the class definition.
Create private fields: int roomNumber and boolean reserved.
Initialize reserved to false in the constructor.
Implement isReserved() to return the reserved field.
Implement reserve() to set reserved to true.
Implement cancelReservation() to set reserved to false.
Override compareTo() from Comparable.
Compare room numbers using Integer.compare(this.roomNumber, other.roomNumber).
